title: A leading oil and natural gas company has entered into an agreement with Accenture to implement the 360° Value framework…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
A leading oil and natural gas company has entered into an agreement with Accenture to implement the 360° Value framework of shared success. How will this framework help the company contribute to shared success?


Transform and reinvent their business and become more profitable.

Transform and reinvent their business, reskill their employees, and become more sustainable.

Become sustainable, improve workplace culture, and reduce their carbon impact.

Reskill their employees, create more job opportunities, and promote gender equality.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
B) transform and reinvent their business, reskill their employees, and become more sustainable. 

The 360° Value framework of shared success helps companies identify and prioritize opportunities for growth and sustainability while also focusing on employee development and reskilling. By implementing this framework, the oil and natural gas company can transform and reinvent their business while also becoming more sustainable. Furthermore, the company can reskill their employees to adapt to new technologies and market trends, contributing to shared success for both the company and its employees.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Match each stage in the value chain against the appropriate action that businesses can take to reduce their carbon impact.

A. Source products and services with a lower carbon footprint. B. Increase efficiency and switch to renewable energy sources. C. Incentivize customer behavior and develop new and more circular business models. D. Use more energy-efficient vehicles and lower-carbon


Logistics; Operations; Customer; Supply chain

Supply chain; Operations; Customer; Logistics

Operations; Customer; Logistics; Supply chain

Supply chain; Customer; Operations; Logistics

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Supply chain; Operations; Customer; Logistics

A. Source products and services with a lower carbon footprint. (Supply chain)

B. Increase efficiency and switch to renewable energy sources. (Operations)

C. Incentivize customer behavior and develop new and more circular business models. (Customer)

D. Use more energy-efficient vehicles and lower-carbon. (Logistics)

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Accenture’s climate leadership strategy comprises four pillars. Match each pillar with the appropriate description.

A. Setting science-based, industry-leading Net Zero and other ambitious sustainability goals B. Working with employees to ensure a greener future C. Involving suppliers in action on climate change D. Partnering with clients and ecosystem partners


Leading by example; Empowering eco-action; Magnifying impact; Innovating

Empowering eco-action; Magnifying impact; Innovating; Leading by example

Leading by example; Magnifying impact; Innovating; Empowering eco-action

Leading by example; Magnifying impact; Empowering eco-action; Innovating

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Leading by example; Empowering eco-action; Magnifying impact; Innovating 

A. Setting science-based, industry-leading Net Zero and other ambitious sustainability goals (Leading by example)

B. Working with employees to ensure a greener future (Empowering eco-action)

C. Involving suppliers in action on climate change (Magnifying impact)

D. Partnering with clients and ecosystem partners (Innovating)

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
The Paris Agreement on climate change legally binds 196 countries to take action on climate change by limiting global average temperature rise to no more than ______ above preindustrial levels.


2°C

3°C

1.5°C

3.1°C–3.7°C

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Is there anything else I can assist you with?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
How are investors and insurers pressurizing organizations to act on climate change?


By prompting demand shifts toward more responsible products, services, and brands

By pushing suppliers and other value chain partners to provide cleaner products and services

By adjusting their portfolios toward companies and products that integrate environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors into their operations and decision-making processes

By framing regulations and policies to force companies into action

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
All of the above actions can be used by investors and insurers to pressure organizations to act on climate change:

- By prompting demand shifts toward more responsible products, services, and brands, investors and insurers can influence companies to prioritize sustainability and consider the environmental impact of their activities.
- By pushing suppliers and other value chain partners to provide cleaner products and services, investors and insurers can indirectly encourage companies to reduce their carbon footprint.
- By adjusting their portfolios toward companies and products that integrate environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors into their operations and decision-making processes, investors and insurers can incentivize companies to address climate risks and opportunities.
- By framing regulations and policies to force companies into action, investors and insurers can create a regulatory environment that supports sustainability practices and encourages companies to take responsibility for their environmental impact.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
A pharmaceutical firm wants to transition to sustainable technologies. As part of this goal, they want to move their applications from enterprise-owned servers to the cloud. What sustainable benefit will they achieve by this migration?


Greater access to finance

Reduced server maintenance effort

Reduced carbon emissions

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The sustainable benefit that the pharmaceutical firm will achieve by migrating their applications from enterprise-owned servers to the cloud is:

Reduced carbon emissions.

This is because cloud infrastructure is designed to be highly energy efficient, with data centers optimized to reduce energy usage and carbon emissions. By moving their applications to the cloud, the pharmaceutical firm can reduce their carbon footprint and contribute to a more sustainable IT ecosystem. Additionally, they can avoid the need to maintain their own servers, which can be resource-intensive and environmentally damaging.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which of the following is an example of a societal benefit of using sustainable technologies?


Reuse of devices using the sharing platform model

Increased transparency in supply chains

Use of renewable technologies in operations

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The example of a societal benefit of using sustainable technologies is:

Reuse of devices using the sharing platform model.

This is because sustainable technologies such as sharing platforms can promote a circular economy by enabling the reuse and repurposing of goods and services, instead of creating waste and pollution. By allowing people to share devices and resources, the sharing platform model can reduce resource consumption and greenhouse gas emissions, while also improving access to affordable solutions. Additionally, it can help to address issues of resource scarcity and promote equitable access to resources, leading to overall societal benefits.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Fill in the blanks: Improved accessibility to data and information, _____________, and greater circularity of hardware and servers are desired customer outcomes of combining sustainable cloud, IT, and software.


increased customer touchpoints

responsible management of data and applications

improved products or services

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Improved accessibility to data and information, responsible management of data and applications, and greater circularity of hardware and servers are desired customer outcomes of combining sustainable cloud, IT, and software.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which of the following is not a key dimension of 360 Value that companies should focus on to achieve their sustainability goals?


Talent

Operational efficiency

Diversity & Inclusion

Financial

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
All three dimensions "Talent", "Operational efficiency" and "Diversity & Inclusion" are key dimensions of 360 Value that companies should focus on to achieve their sustainability goals. 

"Financial" is not a key dimension of 360 Value. However, it is important to keep in mind that financial sustainability is an underlying factor that enables the implementation of sustainability measures across all dimensions of the framework.
